text: IDF confirms killing Ali Yusuf Harshi — Qassem's personal secretary and nephew — in Tallet Khayat Beirut airstrike. Initial Reuters flash claiming Qassem himself killed was retracted. Also killed LRB commander Hamdan in Sidon. Hezbollah has NOT confirmed.
verificationNote: Times of Israel, JPost, Haaretz all confirming IDF statement. Reuters retracted Qassem death claim.

text: Hezbollah has fired approximately 70 rockets from southern Lebanon at northern Israel throughout Day 41 — Kiryat Shmona, Galilee, Taibe, Manara hit. Hezbollah claims response to Israeli ceasefire violations. One rocket struck a home in Kiryat Shmona, no injuries.
verificationNote: IDF and Hezbollah statements confirm. Times of Israel reporting 70+ rockets.

text: BREAKING: Netanyahu orders direct negotiations with Lebanon 'as soon as possible' — focus on Hezbollah disarmament. First meeting at State Dept next week. Decision came after Trump/Witkoff pressure. Israel's Amb Leiter and Lebanon's Amb Hamadeh-Moawad to lead delegations.
verificationNote: Axios and Al Jazeera confirming Netanyahu's office statement.

text: Three Chinese VLCCs testing Hormuz transit: Cospearl Lake, He Rong Hai (Iraqi crude), Yuan Hua Hu (Saudi crude). China FM confirms passage. ZERO oil tankers have transited since ceasefire. Iran demands all ships coordinate with Tehran. 800+ vessels still trapped.
verificationNote: Bloomberg ship-tracking data, Chinese Foreign Ministry, SCMP all confirming.

text: Oil surges on ceasefire doubts: Brent $98.26 (+23.1% pre-war), WTI $100.27 (+32.8%). Goldman flags $100+ Brent if Hormuz shut another month. Mojtaba Khamenei vows 'new phase' for Hormuz. Dow reversing yesterday's +1,325 rally. Gas $4.17/gal holding.
verificationNote: CNBC, Bloomberg, Fortune all confirming oil price data.

text: IntelBrief: 'An Already Tenuous Ceasefire in Iran Hovers on the Verge of Collapse.' Israel launched ~100 airstrikes in 10 minutes across Lebanon. Even if Lebanon formally outside deal, scale of strikes is escalatory. Israel driving wedge between Iran and proxies.
verificationNote: Published on Soufan Center website as official IntelBrief.

text: Lebanon Health Ministry updates April 8 toll: 303 killed, 1,150 wounded (up from initial 254). Total war toll since March 2: 1,888 killed, 6,092 wounded. IDF Division 162 expanding buffer zone in southern Lebanon. Kallas arrives Riyadh for diplomatic push.
verificationNote: CNN live updates, ANI News confirming both figures.

text: CEASEFIRE DAY 3 UPDATE: 21 Israeli strike violations in Lebanon overnight. IRGC confirms Hermes 900 drone shootdown over Fars. Hormuz remains effectively closed — zero tankers transiting. Islamabad talks delegation movements confirmed.
verificationNote: Cross-referenced with Times of Israel liveblog, Al Jazeera, NBC News

text: Wave 73 summary: Hezbollah Ashdod missile (deepest strike attempt) intercepted. IDF 98th Division near-full control of Bint Jbeil. 70+ rockets from Lebanon. Ceasefire holding on Iran front but Lebanon escalating rapidly.
verificationNote: Confirmed via IDF communiqués, Times of Israel

text: Analysis: Israel's 'Operation Eternal Darkness' deliberately timed with ceasefire to maximize pressure. 303 killed in Lebanon on ceasefire Day 1 signals Israel views Hezbollah elimination as separate from Iran deal. Watch for Islamabad talks to address this disconnect.
verificationNote: Expert analysis based on confirmed events

text: BREAKING: IRGC publishes formal mine map via ISNA/Tasnim. Maham-3 and Maham-7 mines confirmed in main Traffic Separation Scheme. Alternative lanes designated north/south of Larak Island. 3 Chinese VLCCs only vessels to transit in 48hrs.
verificationNote: Mine map published by Iranian state news agencies ISNA and Tasnim

text: CENTCOM confirms USS Mason intercepted anti-ship missile targeting Maersk Yorktown in Gulf of Aden. USS Laboon also escorting. Houthis claim additional attacks on MSC vessels. Red Sea/Gulf of Aden shipping threat elevated.
verificationNote: Cross-referenced with CENTCOM release and Wikipedia

text: IMF Georgieva goes full alarm: 'All roads lead to higher prices and slower growth.' Growth downgrade next week. Goldman 30% recession odds. Urea fertilizer at $826/ton (+74% from pre-war). Spring planting crisis looming.
verificationNote: IMF statements confirmed by Al Jazeera, The National

text: Vance delegation confirmed: VP + Witkoff + Kushner heading to Islamabad. Iran delegation: Ghalibaf + Araghchi. Vance says confusion over '3 different 10-point proposals' — one 'probably written by ChatGPT.' Key sticking points: enrichment, Lebanon, Hormuz.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera, Axios, NBC News

text: Mojtaba Khamenei's first public statement since becoming Supreme Leader: 'Resolute in taking revenge.' Still not appearing on camera. Vows 'new phase' for Hormuz management. Statement read by announcer on state TV — no video.
verificationNote: Iranian state TV broadcast confirmed by CBS, CNN

text: March CPI data releasing today (April 10). Economists expect headline +3.4% YoY, +0.9% monthly — steepest monthly rise since June 2022. Oxford Economics projects headline CPI to exceed 4% by April. Gas prices primary driver at +32.5% from pre-war.
verificationNote: Kiplinger, CBS News, Bloomberg confirmed CPI expectations.

text: Islamabad talks status: Pakistan Red Zone sealed, 2-day public holiday declared. VP Vance leading US delegation with Witkoff and Kushner. Iran sending Ghalibaf and Araghchi. PM Sharif mediating. Uncertain if talks proceed after Hormuz re-closure and Lebanon dispute.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, Time, Axios confirmed delegation details.

text: Oil settlement April 9: Brent $95.92, WTI $97.87. Volatile day — Brent hit $100.99 intraday before settling lower. IEA warning April supply crunch 'much worse than March.' Goldman: $100+ Brent all of 2026 if Hormuz shut another month. $115-120 severe scenario.
verificationNote: CNBC, Trading Economics, Fortune, OilPrice.com confirmed.

text: Ceasefire Day 3 scorecard: Hormuz re-closed after Israel Lebanon strikes. Only 5 bulk carriers, ZERO oil tankers transited in 24h. Iran demands all ships coordinate with Tehran. Published mine map (Maham-3/Maham-7). ~800 vessels trapped, 40,000+ seafarers stranded. Hapag-Lloyd: normalization 'weeks away.'
verificationNote: NBC News, CNBC, Bloomberg, Euronews confirmed.

text: Pentagon supplemental request being slashed from $200B to $80-100B per WaPo. CSIS estimates $891M/day for first 100 hours, $28B+ total Pentagon costs. Harvard: $2B/day broader accounting. US 13 KIA, 373 wounded. 950+ Tomahawks used (~$1.9B). Israel war costs ~$15B (NIS 47B).
verificationNote: NPR, CSIS, WaPo, Calcalist confirmed cost figures.

text: UK Foreign Secretary Cooper: Israel Lebanon strikes 'deeply damaging.' EU Kallas in Riyadh pushing for permanent peace. Pope Leo XIV invites prayer vigil at St. Peter's Saturday. Planet Labs imposed indefinite Iran satellite imagery blackout at US request. Chinese firms reportedly running OSINT for Iran.

text: Former Iran FM Kamal Kharazi (81) confirmed dead from April 1 airstrike wounds. His wife was also killed. One of the most senior Iranian officials to die in the conflict. Kharazi served as FM 1997-2005 and was key adviser to Supreme Leader Khamenei.
verificationNote: Free Malaysia Today, TRT World, Al Jazeera confirmed.

text: Saudi-Iran FMs hold first phone call since war began. Prince Faisal bin Farhan and Araghchi discuss 'reducing tensions.' Significant — Saudi balancing Gulf Arab allies with Iran relationship. Comes ahead of Islamabad talks with VP Vance arriving Friday.
verificationNote: The National, Al Arabiya confirmed.

text: Oil surging again: Brent hits $98.27 intraday (+23.1% from pre-war). Ceasefire doubts intensifying as Hormuz remains effectively closed — zero oil tankers through since ceasefire. First non-Iranian tanker (Gabon-flagged) tested transit. Iran demands $2M/vessel transit fee.
verificationNote: CNBC, Trading Economics, Bloomberg confirmed.

text: PBS fact-check: Hegseth's 'historic victory' claims disputed. Joint Chiefs Chairman Caine declined to use 'victory' language. Iran still has nuclear program, Hormuz closed, Hezbollah active. US lost 24 MQ-9 Reapers ($720M+). Pentagon supplemental slashed from $200B to $80-100B.
verificationNote: PBS, WaPo, NPR confirmed.

text: US MQ-9 Reaper losses now at 24 total ($720M+). Iran shot down ~10, others destroyed on ground. Each costs ~$30M. These are backbone of ISR and precision strike ops in Iran. Tomahawk stocks approaching 'Winchester' — 950+ used (~$1.9B). JASSM-ER and GBU-57 MOP also heavily used.
verificationNote: Air & Space Forces, ABC News, 19FortyFive confirmed.

text: Islamabad talks: VP Vance + Witkoff + Kushner arriving today to meet Iran's Parliament Speaker Qalibaf. Highest-level US-Iran meeting since 1979. Key sticking points: enriched uranium, Lebanon inclusion, Hormuz tolls, sanctions. Mojtaba Khamenei: 'new phase' warning. Pope Leo prayer vigil Saturday.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, Axios, NPR confirmed.

text: Mojtaba Khamenei's 'decisive victory' speech is classic face-saving. Iran lost its supreme leader, thousands of IRGC, most of its missile launchers, and multiple nuclear facilities. But Hormuz gives them leverage — and that's what this 'new phase' is about.
verificationNote: Analysis consistent with People's Daily report on Mojtaba's speech and PolitiFact fact-check.

text: Russia/China UNSC veto on Hormuz is the biggest diplomatic play yet. Blocks any legal pathway for Western naval intervention to reopen the strait. Iran now has great-power cover to maintain blockade leverage indefinitely during negotiations.
verificationNote: UNSC veto confirmed by UN News. Analysis tracks with geopolitical dynamics.

text: HRW report on Qasmiyeh Bridge destruction near Tyre is significant — this was southern Lebanon's primary humanitarian corridor. With the bridge gone, 500K+ civilians in the south are effectively cut off from aid. IDF 98th Division has near-full control of Bint Jbeil.
verificationNote: HRW report confirmed. Bridge destruction corroborated by multiple sources.

text: Geolocated: April 10 IDF strikes in Aita al-Shaab and Hanawya in southern Lebanon. Satellite imagery shows residential buildings destroyed. At least 7 killed per Lebanese sources. IDF operating within 10km of the border.
verificationNote: Geolocation confirmed via Al Jazeera reporting and satellite imagery.

text: Key number: ZERO oil tankers have transited Hormuz since ceasefire on April 8. 3 Chinese VLCCs attempted transit but only moved through with special Iran coordination. Iran sea mines remain in place. Ceasefire hasn't changed shipping reality one bit.
verificationNote: Bloomberg confirmed Chinese VLCC transit attempts. NBC confirmed zero oil tanker transits.

text: Lebanon toll on April 8 now confirmed at 303 killed — national mourning day declared. This was Israel's Operation Eternal Darkness, launched HOURS after Iran ceasefire. Netanyahu's message: Iran truce doesn't cover Hezbollah. Most destructive single day since March 2 for Lebanon.
verificationNote: Lebanese Health Ministry figures confirmed. IBTimes reported mourning day.
